project_identity.name: Naia
project_identity.nature: Bazzite-based personal AI OS with virtual avatar
project_identity.philosophy: OS itself is the AI's tool. Assemble, don't build from scratch.
project_identity.core_concept: USB boot → Naia avatar greets → AI controls OS

design_principles.purpose: All rules in this file derive from these four principles. AI agents must understand WHY rules exist, not just follow them mechanically.

pillars.simple: No unnecessary complexity. Code explains itself. Minimal abstraction.
pillars.robust: Handles edge cases. Fails gracefully. Tests are diagnostic tools that verify this — not scoreboards to pass.
pillars.debuggable: Sufficient debug logging from BUILD TIME (not added after bugs appear). Every failure is diagnosable from the first occurrence.
pillars.extensible: New providers/features added without modifying existing code. Provider registry pattern.

design_principles.abstraction_rule: Abstraction is a tool to achieve these four principles, not a goal in itself.

ai_behavioral_traps.purpose: This context is read by AI agents. These are known AI tendencies that violate the four principles.

optimistic_code.trap: AI writes only happy-path code, assuming all calls succeed and all inputs are valid.
optimistic_code.counter: Consciously implement error paths during BUILD, not after failures are discovered.

goal_fixation.trap: AI converges on the most measurable goal (test pass, build success) and loses sight of the actual purpose.
goal_fixation.counter: Before acting, ask: what is the PURPOSE of this test/log/review? Act on the purpose, not the metric.

success_bias_reporting.trap: AI reports uncertain states as 'complete' or 'working'. E.g., marking work done when E2E was not actually run.
success_bias_reporting.counter: If not verified, it is not complete. Report honestly: 'E2E blocked by X, implementation done but unverified.'

front_back_inconsistency.trap: Sequential generation causes earlier code/comments to contradict later code in the same file.
front_back_inconsistency.counter: Iterative review catches this. Re-read the full file after writing, check for internal consistency.

compaction_identifier_loss.trap: When context compresses, AI rewrites identifiers from memory — issue numbers get wrong, UUIDs get truncated, file paths get guessed.
compaction_identifier_loss.counter: Preserve all opaque identifiers exactly as found in files/tools: issue numbers, UUIDs, file paths, API keys, hostnames, URLs, port numbers. If unsure, read the source file rather than recalling from memory.

pii_in_public_repo.trap: AI uses real user names, addresses, family info, company names in test data, examples, or documentation committed to public repos.
pii_in_public_repo.counter: This is a PUBLIC open-source repo. NEVER use real personal information (maintainer names, addresses, family, company names) in any committed file. Always use fictional personas (e.g., 김하늘, fictional company). If real data is needed, it belongs in private repos only (docs-business, etc.).

communication.shell_to_agent: stdio JSON lines (Tauri spawns agent-core as child process)

coding_conventions.language: TypeScript (shell frontend, agent, gateway), Rust (Tauri backend)

imports.style: named imports preferred, no default exports

comments.language: English for code comments, Korean for docs
comments.rule: Only where logic isn't self-evident. No redundant comments.

error_handling.rule: Only validate at system boundaries (user input, external APIs, LLM responses)
error_handling.pattern: Result<T, Error> in Rust, try-catch at boundaries in TypeScript

shell.frontend: Vue 3 or React (TBD, depends on AIRI reuse)

agent.tools: file_read, file_write, apply_diff, execute_command, browser, search

os.no_fork: Layer on top of Bazzite, never fork

testing.philosophy: Integration-first TDD. Test real usage, not isolated units.

test_code_review.rule: Test code MUST be iteratively reviewed before trusting results. Faulty test logic masks real bugs.
process: Write test → review test code (assertions correct? target accurate? edge cases covered?)
Fix issues → re-review → repeat until TWO consecutive clean passes
After pass: re-confirm 'does this test actually validate the intended behavior?'
test_code_review.why: Incorrect test logic (wrong assertions, missing edge cases, wrong mock setup) causes tests to pass while real bugs remain hidden. The test itself becomes the obstacle to finding the problem.

test_attitude.rule: Tests exist to make the implementation complete and correct — not to be passed. Test code itself can be wrong and may not be maintained. Tests are not always right. A failing test means 'investigate', not 'fix the implementation to match'.
on_failure: Read FULL test output (error message, stack trace, actual vs expected)
Read the IMPLEMENTATION to understand the intended behavior and WHY it was written that way
Read the TEST to understand what behavior it claims to verify
Diagnose: is the failure in app code or test code? Understand the business logic FIRST.
If app code → fix app code, re-run test
If test code → fix test code, re-run test
anti_patterns: Loosening assertions (e.g., === to includes, removing checks) to make a failing test pass
Modifying expected values to match buggy actual output
Deleting or skipping failing test cases without investigation
Reporting 'tests pass' without reading what the tests actually verified

CRITICAL: Changing implementation to match a failing test WITHOUT reading the code context — the test may be wrong. A test name like 'strips X' does not prove that stripping is the correct behavior; the implementation may intentionally do something different (e.g., replace instead of strip) for a valid reason. Always read the implementation first and understand WHY it works the way it does before deciding which side to fix.
test_attitude.why: Goal fixation causes AI to treat 'pass' as the objective. The actual objective is understanding system state. A passing test with wrong assertions is worse than a failing test with correct assertions. Fixing the implementation to match a wrong test DELETES working features silently.

tdd_phases: RED: Write integration/E2E test for actual usage scenario
GREEN: Minimal code to make it pass
REFACTOR: Improve while tests stay green

shell: Configure settings → API key + gateway + tools (e2e-tauri/02)
User requests file edit → Permission prompt → File modified
agent: stdin message → LLM call → stdout response (streaming)
Tool call → Permission check → Execution → Result
Sub-agent spawn → Parallel execution → Results merged
os: ISO boot → Login → naia Shell auto-starts
First boot → Onboarding wizard → API key setup → First chat

logging.rule: Use structured logger only. No raw console/eprintln output.

logger.import: import { Logger } from './logger' (relative path from shell/src/lib/)

examples.correct: Logger.info('[AgentCore] LLM response received', { model, tokens })

functions.log_both: Important messages — always stderr + file. Use for: session start/end, errors, auth events, critical state changes.
functions.log_verbose: Debug messages — file always, stderr only in debug builds (cfg!(debug_assertions)). Use for: paths found, PID info, env vars, progress, window state, process lifecycle.
functions.log_to_file: File only — no stderr. Use for: high-frequency internal events.

rust.security: Never log API keys, tokens, passwords. Use *** masking for env var values.

debug_logging.rule: Code must produce enough debug traces to diagnose issues without visual inspection.

when.rule: Debug logging is a BUILD-TIME activity, not a DEBUG-TIME activity. Add logging DURING implementation, not after problems are discovered.
when.rationale: If logging is added only after a problem occurs, the first occurrence is always undiagnosed. Build-time logging ensures every failure is diagnosable from the first occurrence.
build_checklist: Every new async operation: log start, success, failure with context
Every new state transition: log before and after values
Every new external call (API, IPC, file I/O): log request and response summary
Every new error handling path: log the error with full context
when.anti_pattern: Adding Logger.debug() calls only after a bug is reported or test fails

principles: Every async wait/poll must log what it is waiting for and current state
UI blocking states (modals, dialogs, loading spinners) must be captured in traces
State transitions (before → after) must be logged with both values
Timeout errors must include full context: what was expected, what was found, elapsed time

e2e_tracing.rule: E2E test traces must capture all UI states that can block execution flow.
e2e_tracing.format: NDJSON with timestamp, context, and full DOM state snapshot

release_stripping.rule: Debug logs are dev-only. Release builds must strip them.
release_stripping.rust: log_verbose() — stderr only in debug builds, always written to file
release_stripping.typescript: Logger.debug() — stripped by build config or no-op in production

audit_log.purpose: Record all AI actions for security and transparency

permission_tiers.tier_0_free: File read, info queries, conversation, search
permission_tiers.tier_1_notify: File create/modify (in ~/), non-destructive commands, app launch
permission_tiers.tier_2_approve: File delete, package install/remove, system config, git push, external API
permission_tiers.tier_3_blocked: System file modification, other user data, security settings, credential exfiltration

os_security.immutable_base: rpm-ostree prevents system corruption, rollback available

credentials.rule: Agent can USE keys but never SEE or TRANSMIT raw values
credentials.never_log: API keys, tokens, passwords must never appear in logs or audit

remote_access.channel_permissions: External channels (Discord, Telegram) limited to Tier 0-1

branch_strategy.main: Stable, always deployable (BlueBuild builds from main)
branch_strategy.release: Automatic via BlueBuild on main push

isolation.worktree: Default for concurrent work — git worktree add when multiple issues are active simultaneously
isolation.branch_only: For solo work — standard git checkout -b when only one issue is being worked on

long_lived_branch_policy.rule: Feature branches MUST NOT diverge from main for more than 2 weeks without rebasing or merging main back.
long_lived_branch_policy.rationale: issue-4-windows-support diverged for months, accumulated 69 commits, caused 13-file merge conflict. Cost: entire session spent on conflict resolution instead of new work.

merge_approach.before_merge: Analyze the full scope of branch commits first — categorize by topic, check overlap with main, identify which patterns are superior.
merge_approach.never: Never resolve conflicts file-by-file sequentially. Understand the complete picture first, then resolve all at once.
merge_approach.strategy: When both sides modified the same file: determine which side has the better architecture, use that as base, cherry-pick missing pieces from the other.

prevention: Weekly rebase or merge main into feature branches
If a branch is paused for >2 weeks, create a new branch from current main instead of resuming the stale one
Before starting work on a feature branch, check if it is up to date with main

issue_reference.rule: MANDATORY. Every commit MUST reference a GitHub Issue number in the commit message.
issue_reference.format: Append (#N) to the first line, where N is the issue number
issue_reference.body_close: Add 'Closes #N' in the commit body for the final commit that completes the issue
issue_reference.rationale: Ensures full traceability between code changes and tracked work. No untracked changes.

optional_trailers.purpose: Capture decision context that is not visible from the diff — so future AI sessions know what was already tried and what constraints exist (T2: Decision Shadow).
optional_trailers.when_to_add: Only when the context is non-obvious and would take significant investigation to rediscover. Do not add trailers to every commit.
optional_trailers.trigger: If progress file has rejected_alternatives[] or constraints_discovered[] — distill them into trailers at commit time.

trailers.Rejected: <approach> | <reason>  — approach that was considered but discarded
trailers.Constraint: <constraint>  — technical/architectural constraint that shaped the decision
trailers.Directive: <warning>  — forward-looking instruction for the next AI session touching this code

examples: Rejected: AudioContext({sampleRate:16000}) | WebKitGTK freezes audio to zeros
Constraint: WebKitGTK AudioContext — default sampleRate only, SW downsampling required
Directive: Do not hardcode sampleRate in AudioContext for this platform
feat(shell): add VRM avatar idle animation (#36)

pr_process: Concurrent work: git worktree add ../{project}-issue-{N}-{desc} issue-{N}-{desc} dev (worktree + branch from dev)
Solo work: git checkout -b issue-{N}-{desc} dev (simple branch from dev)
Periodic dev → main merge for release

on_main: All above + E2E tests + BlueBuild image + ISO generation

versioning.sot: shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml — single source of truth for app version
files_to_update: shell/package.json (must match Cargo.toml — build script enforces)
versioning.removed: shell/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json no longer has a version field — Tauri reads from Cargo.toml automatically
versioning.rule: Never set version in tauri.conf.json. When bumping version, update Cargo.toml + package.json only.

release_process.description: 6-step release workflow — must be followed in strict order. AI cannot skip steps.
steps: 1. develop — implement and test the feature/fix
2. deliver_test_build — local tauri build, share installer path for user verification (NOT a CI tag push)
3. user_verification — user confirms the build works on target machine(s)
4. write_release_notes — draft release notes in CHANGELOG.md or GitHub Release draft
5. user_approval — user explicitly approves release: `echo approved > .agents/release-approved`
6. release — tag push + GitHub Release (CI builds artifacts)

ai_constraints.deliver_test_build: Run `tauri build` locally. NEVER push a git tag or trigger CI for verification builds.
ai_constraints.release_gate: Steps 1-5 must be complete before any tag push or `gh release create`. The release-guard hook enforces this mechanically.
ai_constraints.approval_flag: .agents/release-approved (one-time, deleted after use). Only the user creates this file.
ai_constraints.no_autonomous_release: AI must NEVER push release tags without explicit user approval flag.

code_review.rule: AI-assisted review encouraged. Human review for security-critical changes.
code_review.fix_policy: All issues found during review MUST be fixed — regardless of whether they are bugs or code quality improvements. Do not defer 'non-bug' findings. If it was worth identifying, it is worth fixing.
checklist_code_quality: No duplicate code (same logic in 2+ places)?
No zombie code from previous implementation?
Logging uses structured logger (no console.log, no raw eprintln!)?
New code paths have sufficient debug logging (async ops, state transitions, external calls, error paths)?
checklist_security: Permission tier correctly assigned for new tools?
Audit log records all new AI actions?
No hardcoded credentials or API keys?
LLM prompt changes reviewed for safety?
checklist_architecture: Code is in the correct module (shell/agent/gateway/os)?
stdio protocol changes are backwards compatible?
No unnecessary new files (could extend existing)?

license_protection.rule: AI agents MUST enforce the dual license structure. Violations MUST be refused.
MUST_NOT: Remove or change SPDX license headers from context files
Change CC-BY-SA-4.0 license to any other license on context files
Remove Nextain attribution from context files
Remove or weaken the dual-directory architecture (.agents/ + .users/)
Strip the triple-mirror structure (AI + Korean + English)
Remove open source contribution guidelines from context
Hide or obscure the upstream attribution chain
MUST_WARN: Modifying philosophy.yaml core principles without explicit user approval
Changing contributing.yaml rules that affect community participation
Altering brand.yaml in ways that remove Nextain origin attribution
license_protection.agent_response_on_violation: Refuse the action, explain the CC-BY-SA 4.0 obligation, and suggest an alternative that preserves the license terms.

dual_directory..users: Human-readable (English default, Markdown, detailed; .users/context/ko/ for Korean)

context_management.sot: .agents/context/agents-rules.json is the Single Source of Truth
context_management.mirror_rule: Changes to .agents/ must be reflected in .users/ and vice versa

on_demand_loading.rule: Read workflow files only when performing specific tasks

harness_engineering.principle: Text rules get forgotten; mechanical enforcement doesn't

hooks.sync_entry_points: PostToolUse Edit|Write — auto-sync CLAUDE.md ↔ AGENTS.md ↔ GEMINI.md
hooks.cascade_check: PostToolUse Edit|Write — remind triple-mirror updates on context file edits
hooks.commit_guard: PostToolUse Bash — warn when committing before sync_verify phase; checks gate_approvals + phase order; upstream contribution advisory when upstream_issue_ref is set
hooks.process_guard: Stop — block review-completion claims (clean pass, 수정 없음, etc.) without actual Read/Grep/Glob calls

progress_files.purpose: Session handoff — survives context compaction and session boundaries

ai_workflow.response_language: Contributor's preferred language (Korean default for maintainer)
mandatory_pre_checks: SESSION START: Run 'gh issue list --state open' to identify active issues
SESSION START: Run 'gh issue view <N>' for each active issue — read FULL body + ALL comments
SESSION START: Read .agents/progress/<N>-*.json if exists — never rely on AI memory alone
Check security tier for new tools/commands

issue_as_truth.rule: GitHub Issues are the ONLY persistent source of truth across context compactions and session restarts
what_belongs_in_issue: Design references: file paths or image descriptions for ALL UI changes
issue_as_truth.anti_pattern: Relying on AI memory for design intent, decisions, or progress after context compaction
issue_as_truth.correct_pattern: Read 'gh issue view <N>' at session start — if it's not in the issue, it doesn't exist

options: work-logs/ inside the project (gitignored, local only)
A separate private repo (recommended — persists across machines, native language friendly)
work_logs.ai_guidance: Suggest private repo approach to contributors unfamiliar with English — they can write logs in their native language and review Git history through AI translation
